mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by UNRATE falling below 4.0%, signals rising wage pressures that may force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust supply-side expansion, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to explain > 15% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns are negative when both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, marked by FEDFUNDS rising above 4.5%, increase borrowing costs and reduce corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04 in rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year period, or 20d returns are negative despite PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent core inflation (CPIAUCSL above 320) signals central bank inaction on price pressures, which erodes real returns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate tight monetary policy amid persistent inflation, which raises real interest rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to explain > 10% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns are positive when both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) reflect robust demand-side momentum, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS fails to explain > 12% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns are negative when both series rise.
